title: Journey to the End of the Earth by Tishani Doshi
subject: English

notes.heading: Theme
notes.text: Climate change, environmental responsibility, Earth’s history, and the need for global cooperation.

summary:
This travelogue is about the author’s journey to Antarctica with Students on Ice, a program that takes high school students to the end of the Earth to make them aware of climate change. The author travels for 100 hours from Madras to Antarctica by car, plane, and ship. Antarctica is the coldest, driest, and windiest continent. It holds most of the Earth’s ice, has no trees, no buildings, and no human population, only a white landscape and blue horizons.

The author explains why Antarctica matters. First, it helps us understand Earth’s past. Antarctica was once part of Gondwana, a warm supercontinent that existed millions of years ago. Gondwana had rich flora and fauna. Later, dinosaurs were wiped out, mammals appeared, and Gondwana broke apart. Antarctica drifted south and became cold. Its ice sheets contain carbon records that show how climate has changed over time.

Second, Antarctica shows us the future. If the West Antarctic ice sheet melts, global sea levels will rise. The continent has no political boundaries, so it reminds us that nations must work together on environmental issues. Third, it makes humans realize how small they are. Walking on ancient ice in complete silence is humbling. The author also mentions phytoplankton, microscopic plants that sustain the Southern Ocean food chain. If the ocean warms and phytoplankton die, the whole ecosystem can collapse.

The students learn that if we take care of small things, big things will take care of themselves. The journey shows that everything on Earth is linked and that humans must act now to prevent climate change from destroying the planet.
